text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little town named Everwood, there was a dog named Winston. Winston was no ordinary dog—he could talk. Everybody loved Winston because his voice was so witty. Every morning, he'd bark something amusing like, "Morning! Hope you've got something tasty on your plate!"

One particular summer day, with no birds singing and just a single squirrel nibbling on an acorn, Winston decided to host a conclave to talk about the woes of suburbia. He gathered the town's smartest animals, a rabbit named Harvey, a wise old owl named Olive, and even a fish named Finnick.

They went to Winston's deck, where a garden bloomed with flowers and a large book bowl full of mystery novels sat gathering rust.

"What's the biggest worry we have?" Winston asked the gathered animals.

"Well, there's traffic!" Harvey chirped. "Those cars... they could kill us!"

"Fear not!" said Olive with her velvet voice
